findings.description: attempt is reset to 0 at the top of every loop iteration before the increment at line 71, so the while attempt < MAX_RETRIES guard never advances and a persistently failing job loops forever instead of stopping after 3 tries.
subprocess.run with shell=True on an f-string built from attacker-controlled payload source/dest fields is textbook command injection (e.g. dest='; rm -rf / #').
Blocking time.sleep is called inside the async poll_loop coroutine, freezing the entire event loop on every empty poll and every retry backoff instead of awaiting asyncio.sleep.
Because attempt is pinned at 0 by the line-69 reset, the backoff term RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (attempt + 1) is always 2.0, so the intended exponential/linear backoff never escalates.
poll_loop's while True has no CancelledError handling or stop signal, so when stop_after is None it can never be cleanly terminated.
A job that raises is caught and logged but then falls into the same broken attempt-reset path, so exceptions also trigger the infinite loop rather than counting toward retry exhaustion.
JobQueue.pop uses list.pop(0), an O(n) operation that reshuffles the whole list on each dequeue; collections.deque would give O(1) FIFO.
